title: strychnos subcordata
intro: Strychnos subcordata, also known as the subcordate strychnine tree or the bush apple tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Africa and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Strychnos subcordata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote digestive health, making it a popular herb among local communities. Herbal preparations of Strychnos subcordata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to extract its medicinal compounds and make them accessible for therapeutic use.
